channel.title: Analysis: Our new Edge and Dev infrastructure (Vercel)
channel.description:
https://vercel.com/blog/new-edge-dev-infrastructure
They announce a set of improvements in several tools and infrastructure which provides a better general developer experience
## NextJs
- Faster reload in development mode
## Vercer CLI
- Faster - Serverles functions can be tested locally - Environment variables taken from the cloud
## Infrastructure
- Vercel's own Anycast IP range (fastest ones, like Google Public DNS → 8.8.8.8) for all deployments - More secure

item.title: Analysis: Our new Edge and Dev infrastructure (Vercel)
item.dc:creator: juanma
item.description:
The article has been written by these profiles in the company:

  • Matheus Fernandes @matheusfrndes → Head of Infra @vercel
  • Nathan Rajlich @tootallnate → Programmer / Nerd. Engineer @vercel
  • Tim Neutkens @timneutkens → Next.js lead @vercel, co-author of Next.js,
  • Joe Haddad @timer150 → Sr Software Engineer at ▲Vercel, working on Next.js.
  • Max Leiter @max_leiter → CS/BA @USC, Intern @vercel

Read full topic
item.source: Analysis: Our new Edge and Dev infrastructure (Vercel)

item.title: Analysis: Our new Edge and Dev infrastructure (Vercel)
item.dc:creator: juanma
item.description:
vercel.com

Our new Edge and Dev infrastructure – Vercel

Introducing major end-to-end enhancements from a better development experience to serving pages even faster.

They announce a set of improvements in several tools and infrastructure which provides a better general developer experience

NextJs

  • Faster reload in development mode

Vercer CLI

  • Faster
  • Serverles functions can be tested locally
  • Environment variables taken from the cloud

Infrastructure

  • Vercel’s own Anycast IP range (fastest ones, like Google Public DNS → 8.8.8.8) for all deployments
  • More secure

Read full topic
item.source: Analysis: Our new Edge and Dev infrastructure (Vercel)
